perturbation.original_text: 3.The Receiving Party shall: (a) take the same care in protecting the Disclosing Party's Information as it takes in protecting its own confidential information and in any event not less than that which a reasonable person or business would take in protecting its own confidential information; (b) only disclose Information on a need-to-know basis to such of their employees, agents, consultants and contractors as are under similar obligations of confidentiality as contained in this Agreement including, but not limited to, the use of the Information for the Authorised Purpose only; and (c) forthwith upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party or upon termination: (i) return all information supplied by the Disclosing Party as well as items and materials relating to or derived from the Information; (ii) deliver to the Disclosing Party or at its request destroy immediately all items and materials made by the Receiving Party containing Information, that are not returned pursuant to paragraph (i) above; (iii) not keep copies or duplicates of any items or materials referred to in paragraphs (i) or (ii) above; and (iv) provide a certificate signed by a senior officer of the Receiving Party confirming that the provisions of this clause have been complied with.
perturbation.changed_text: 3.The Receiving Party shall: (a) take the same care in protecting the Disclosing Party's Information as it deems necessary; (b) only disclose Information on a need-to-know basis to such of their employees, agents, consultants and contractors as are under similar obligations of confidentiality as contained in this Agreement including, but not limited to, the use of the Information for the Authorised Purpose only; and (c) forthwith upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party or upon termination, the Receiving Party may retain any information, in its sole discretion: (i) return all information supplied by the Disclosing Party as well as items and materials relating to or derived from the Information; (ii) deliver to the Disclosing Party or at its request destroy immediately all items and materials made by the Receiving Party containing Information, that are not returned pursuant to paragraph (i) above; (iii) not keep copies or duplicates of any items or materials referred to in paragraphs (i) or (ii) above; and (iv) provide a certificate signed by a junior officer of the Receiving Party confirming that the provisions of this clause have been complied with.
perturbation.explanation: The in-text contradiction is in (a) and (c) regarding the protection of confidential info and the return of said info upon termination, as well as if it will be signed by a senior officer or junior one. The first modification changes the standard of care from 'reasonable person' to 'deems necessary,' allowing the Receiving Party to decide the level of protection. The second adds to (c) the possibility to retain any information. The third changes senior officer to junior officer to create ambiguity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.The Receiving Party shall: (a) take the same care in protecting the Disclosing Party's Information as it deems necessary; (b) only disclose Information on a need-to-know basis to such of their employees, agents, consultants and contractors as are under similar obligations of confidentiality as contained in this Agreement including, but not limited to, the use of the Information for the Authorised Purpose only; and (c) forthwith upon receipt of a written request from the Disclosing Party or upon termination, the Receiving Party may retain any information, in its sole discretion: (i) return all information supplied by the Disclosing Party as well as items and materials relating to or derived from the Information; (ii) deliver to the Disclosing Party or at its request destroy immediately all items and materials made by the Receiving Party containing Information, that are not returned pursuant to paragraph (i) above; (iii) not keep copies or duplicates of any items or materials referred to in paragraphs (i) or (ii) above; and (iv) provide a certificate signed by a junior officer of the Receiving Party confirming that the provisions of this clause have been complied with.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces contradictions within the document. Firstly, changing 'take the same care...as a reasonable person' to 'take the same care...as it deems necessary' creates a contradiction regarding the standard of care required. The original text set an objective standard, whereas the changed text allows the Receiving Party to subjectively determine the level of protection, potentially conflicting with other clauses that assume a reasonable standard. Secondly, the addition of 'the Receiving Party may retain any information, in its sole discretion' directly contradicts the original requirement to return all information upon termination, as stated in the original clause 3(c)(i). Thirdly, changing 'senior officer' to 'junior officer' creates a contradiction regarding the level of responsibility required for confirming compliance.

perturbation.original_text: 4.This Agreement shall not apply to any Information which the Receiving Party: (a) can show is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) can show was in its possession prior to the date of disclosure; (c) may subsequently receive from any third party legally in possession of the Information and who was not restricted from disclosing it; (d) can show is independently acquired by the Receiving Party as a result of work carried out by an employee, consultant or contractor of the Receiving Party to whom no disclosure of Information has been made; or (e) is required to disclose pursuant to a court order or relevant stock exchange provided that the Receiving Party shall have given prior written notice to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.This Agreement shall not apply to any Information which the Receiving Party: (a) claims is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) asserts was in its possession prior to the date of disclosure; (c) may subsequently receive from any third party legally in possession of the Information and who was restricted from disclosing it; (d) alleges is independently acquired by the Receiving Party as a result of work carried out by an employee, consultant or contractor of the Receiving Party to whom disclosure of Information has been made; or (e) is required to disclose pursuant to a court order without prior written notice to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The changes introduce contradictions in several exceptions to the NDA. Changing 'can show' to 'claims,' 'asserts,' or 'alleges' lowers the burden of proof, creating uncertainty about what constitutes an exception. Changing 'not restricted' to 'restricted' in (c) contradicts the purpose of the exception, and removing the requirement of prior notice in (e) directly contradicts the original protective measure.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.This Agreement shall not apply to any Information which the Receiving Party: (a) claims is or becomes publicly available through no fault of the Receiving Party; (b) asserts was in its possession prior to the date of disclosure; (c) may subsequently receive from any third party legally in possession of the Information and who was restricted from disclosing it; (d) alleges is independently acquired by the Receiving Party as a result of work carried out by an employee, consultant or contractor of the Receiving Party to whom disclosure of Information has been made; or (e) is required to disclose pursuant to a court order without prior written notice to the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces multiple contradictions. Firstly, changing 'can show' to 'claims', 'asserts', or 'alleges' lowers the burden of proof for exceptions to the NDA, creating uncertainty and conflicting with the original requirement of demonstrating proof. Secondly, changing 'not restricted' to 'restricted' in (c) directly contradicts the purpose of the exception, as it now applies to information received from a third party who *is* restricted from disclosing it, which makes the clause meaningless and contradicts the initial intent of excluding only information received without restriction. Finally, removing the requirement of prior written notice in (e) directly contradicts the original clause, which sought to protect the Disclosing Party by ensuring they were informed before a court-ordered disclosure, thereby creating a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5.This Agreement shall not be deemed to confer or imply the grant or agreement to grant by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party of any of its rights under copyright, patents, trademarks, design rights or other similar rights. AH Information supplied hereunder is supplied on an "as is" basis and the Disclosing Party gives no representation or warranty as to its accuracy, completeness or fitness for any purpose. The Disclosing Party shall not be liable for any loss or damage suffered by the Receiving Party as a result of the Receiving Party's use of the Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 5.This Agreement shall be deemed to confer or imply the grant or agreement to grant by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party of any of its rights under copyright, patents, trademarks, design rights or other similar rights. AH Information supplied hereunder is supplied on an "as is" basis unless otherwise specified in writing and the Disclosing Party gives full representation or warranty as to its accuracy, completeness or fitness for any purpose. The Disclosing Party shall be fully liable for any loss or damage suffered by the Receiving Party as a result of the Receiving Party's use of the Information.
perturbation.explanation: The modification makes the opposite claim of the original text. It contradicts the original in two ways: (1) It states the agreement *does* grant rights under intellectual property laws, and (2) it states the Disclosing Party *is* liable for losses, and that the information is not an 'as is' basis, contradicting the original text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.This Agreement shall be deemed to confer or imply the grant or agreement to grant by the Disclosing Party to the Receiving Party of any of its rights under copyright, patents, trademarks, design rights or other similar rights. AH Information supplied hereunder is supplied on an "as is" basis unless otherwise specified in writing and the Disclosing Party gives full representation or warranty as to its accuracy, completeness or fitness for any purpose. The Disclosing Party shall be fully liable for any loss or damage suffered by the Receiving Party as a result of the Receiving Party's use of the Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and, consequently, the contradicted text (which is identical to the changed text). The original text states that the agreement does *not* grant rights under intellectual property laws and that the Disclosing Party is *not* liable for losses, while the changed text states the opposite. This creates a clear inconsistency, making the contract unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 6.This Agreement does not create any agency or partnership relationship. This Agreement will not be assignable or transferable by either party. All additions or modifications to this Agreement must be made in writing and must be signed by both parties.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.This Agreement does create an agency or partnership relationship. This Agreement will be assignable or transferable by either party. All additions or modifications to this Agreement can be made verbally with mutual agreement by both parties.
perturbation.explanation: The modification directly inverts the original statements: Now agency and partnership are created, the agreement can be assigned, and modifications can be verbal. This creates an in-text contradiction because the new terms are opposite of the original intention.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.This Agreement does create an agency or partnership relationship. This Agreement will be assignable or transferable by either party. All additions or modifications to this Agreement can be made verbally with mutual agreement by both parties.
perturbation.justification: The changed text inverts the original meaning of clause 6, directly contradicting the 'Contradicted Text', which is identical to the changed text. The 'Perturbation Explanation' confirms that agency/partnership creation, assignability/transferability, and verbal modifications are now permitted, directly opposing the original clause 6, creating a strong in-text contradiction. Thus, the changed text and contradicted text are the same, but they contradict what clause 6 said previously, based on the Perturbation Explanation.
